name: SEC EDGAR MCP Server
what_it_does: Community MCP server providing AI assistants real-time access to official SEC EDGAR filings and financial data. Enables agents to search and retrieve 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K filings, earnings data, and SEC-registered company information from the SEC's public EDGAR database — essential for financial research and investment analysis workflows.
use_cases: Accessing SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K) for financial research agents
Retrieving earnings data and company financial statements from investment analysis agents
Monitoring SEC filings for specific companies from compliance tracking agents
Research on SEC-registered companies and their regulatory disclosures from due diligence agents
not_for: Non-US financial markets (SEC EDGAR is US-specific)
Real-time trading signals (SEC filings are regulatory disclosures, not price feeds)
Automated trading based on filing data without human oversight
best_when: A financial research, investment analysis, or compliance agent needs to access official SEC regulatory filings — 10-K annual reports, 10-Q quarterly filings, 8-K material events — from the authoritative SEC EDGAR source.
avoid_when: You need non-US public company filings or real-time market data. EDGAR is specifically for SEC-registered US public companies.

auth.notes: No authentication required — SEC EDGAR is a free public API. User-Agent header required per SEC EDGAR API terms.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — SEC EDGAR is a public government database
pricing.notes: SEC EDGAR is free public data. MCP server is free open source. No costs to query EDGAR.

performance.rate_limits: SEC EDGAR: 10 requests/second; identify yourself via User-Agent header

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: SEC filing data used in investment or compliance decisions — verify data accuracy before acting
SEC EDGAR rate limit is 10 req/second — bulk filing retrieval needs throttling; identify via User-Agent
SEC EDGAR HTML filings can be large and complex — parser performance varies by filing structure
Community server (plainyogurt21) — verify compatibility with EDGAR API v2 for newest filing types
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Public financial data but used in investment contexts. No auth required. HTTPS to EDGAR. Provide valid User-Agent per SEC EDGAR terms of service.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public government data. No auth. HTTPS. Financial research context — validate outputs. User-Agent required by SEC.
